rationale: Neither candidate surfaces the one substantive ground-truth concern (dsocek's note about driving naming-conflict handling from _packed_modules_mapping), which likely referenced a hunk removed before the final diff, so recall is low and roughly equal for both. X's two findings (derive default_components from _dit_modules; text-encoder LoRA is a silent no-op) are well-grounded with specific file:line evidence and coherent, non-contradictory verdicts. Y raises a near-duplicate of X's first point plus a second finding explicitly hedged as speculative ('if true... likely harmless'), triple-repeats the same review text (formatting bug), and issues a self-contradictory verdict (REQUEST CHANGES header vs. 'Approve and merge' next_action), undermining precision and actionability.
